title: ARCHIVED – Notice - Protecting vulnerable temporary foreign workers - Canada.ca
content:
## ARCHIVED – Notice - Protecting vulnerable temporary foreign workers

## We have archived this page and will not be updating it.
You can use it for research or reference.

## We have archived this page and will not be updating it.
You can use it for research or reference.
July 24, 2012 — To protect vulnerable foreign workers from the risk of abuse and exploitation, effective July 14, 2012, foreign nationals issued new work permits are restricted from working in sex trade-related businesses, such as strip clubs, escort services and massage parlours.
To find out more information please see related links below:
- Operational Bulletin 449 – Work Permit applications for Temporary Foreign Workers for businesses in sectors where there are reasonable grounds to suspect a risk of sexual exploitation of some workers – archived [ 13 July 2012 ]
- Government of Canada takes action to protect temporary foreign workers [ 4 July 2012 ]
